page: Microsoft Azure F2 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the F server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 33 GB of local storage. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in this profile.

Performance benchmarks place the F2 instance in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute resources. Synthetic CPU evaluations, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, consistently rank in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 131.23 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 14.41 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic and database operations, such as Passmark database tests and Redis SET operations, register in the bottom 10% to 25% performance brackets.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the F2 is structured as an entry-level compute-optimized instance. The primary tradeoff is its constrained memory capacity of 4.0 GB and lower-tier benchmark results across processing and memory bandwidth. This server is best suited for lightweight workloads that require dedicated CPU cores but have minimal memory footprints, such as basic web serving, simple utility scripting, or small-scale development and testing environments.
description: Microsoft Azure F2 is an entry-level, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and 33 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPU. Benchmark evaluations place its processing, memory bandwidth, and database performance in the lower tiers, typically within the bottom 10% to 25% of tested servers. Due to its balanced but constrained hardware profile and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the F2 is qualitatively cost-efficient for low-demand, dedicated compute tasks. It is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, small-scale development environments, and simple background utility scripts.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 33 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight dedicated workloads.

tagline: An entry-level, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors and local storage for lightweight, memory-constrained workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory and 33 GB of local storage
Compute-optimized 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio on Hyper-V hypervisor
